_doc:
Phase 9 demo — elaboration: AST → IR with width inference + parameter resolution.

Phase 8 turned text into an AST; Phase 9 lowers the AST into the same IR the exporter produces from a circuit. The two pipelines now meet in the middle:

  circuit.json ─ exportCircuit ─→ Verilog text ─ parseVerilog ─→ AST ─ elaborate ─→ IR
         \                                                                       /
          ╰─ fromCircuit ────────────────────────────→ IR (same shape)  ←──────╯

This scene exercises every elaboration responsibility:
  • Parameter resolution — a parameter governs the bit-width of d/q/mem.
  • Width inference — every IR Ref / Slice / Concat carries an explicit width derived from the symbol table.
  • Always blocks — sensitivity lists and statement bodies (if/case/blocking/non-blocking) all lower 1:1.
  • Memory pattern — `reg [W-1:0] mem [0:DEPTH-1]` becomes IR.memories.
  • (* attribute *) blocks — strings carried through to IR.attributes as { key: 'verilog-attr', value }.
  • Submodule instantiation — child module goes under IR.submodules.

Run `node examples/tests/test-hdl-elaborate.mjs` for the unit suite (30 checks) or `node examples/tests/test-hdl-elaborate-l1-gate.mjs` for the end-to-end round-trip on every Phase 1-8 demo (21/21 pass: ports, nets, memories, instance counts all match the exporter's IR).
